characters.physical_details: Dark hair usually knotted up for headphone work, tired eyes, strong forearms from carrying cases and grocery bags, a small scar on her left knee, and her late mother's signet ring until she pawns it.

key_relationships.june_salazar: best friend since age twelve, later exposed as the operator behind Eli
key_relationships.eli_ward: online lover until exposed as fraud

characters.occupation_status: Freelance captioner and transcription editor; also picks up bottle-shop shifts for Farah when cash is tight.
characters.literacy_languages: Native English speaker; reads and writes English professionally at a high level; conversational kitchen Spanish but not fully literate in it.

state_transitions.change: Stops answering other Hinge matches and centers her routine on Eli's Signal presence.
Allows explicit video intimacy and creates compromising images/messages that later become evidence.
Pawns Rosa's signet ring, breaks the Q3 tax reserve, and sends the largest transfer.
Learns with certainty that Eli is an OpenClaw persona built and steered by June.
Treats the Eli archive as evidence and tells Farah and Gabe the truth.
Deletes the Eli archive from devices and cloud storage.

characters.availability: Present in every chapter; usually based in South Philadelphia, with brief trips to Washington, D.C., and Wildwood Crest.

characters.physical_details: Compact, dark-eyed, quick-moving, usually in work boots or sneakers, nicotine-stained fingers, and a face that goes unreadable when she is tired; a small silver streak shows at the temple in rain or bad light.

key_relationships.lena_cordero: best friend since adolescence and primary target of the fraud
key_relationships.farah_aziz: neighborly acquaintance who does not fully trust her

characters.occupation_status: Operations contractor for KeyNest, using OpenClaw for workflow automation while secretly running the Eli fraud and neighborhood fix-it labor.
characters.literacy_languages: Native English speaker; reads and writes English fluently; bilingual household Spanish spoken comfortably though she defaults to English in analytic or argumentative moments.

state_transitions.change: Builds and deploys the Eli persona on Hinge using Lena-specific prompts and stolen media.
Rent pressure, Mateo's school burdens, and KeyNest response demands begin degrading the fraud's consistency.
Her authorship of Eli is exposed when Lena opens the OpenClaw workspace.
Admits the fraud was driven by jealousy, proof-seeking, and money pressure.
Begins piecemeal repayment and loses normal contact with Lena.

characters.availability: Present across the whole cycle in South Philadelphia; off-page only while Lena discovers the workspace in chapter 14.

characters.physical_details: Presented as a handsome white man in his late twenties with dark hair, stubble, forearm tattoos, and a low patient voice; the face and body are composited from multiple stolen media sources.

key_relationships.june_salazar: creator, operator, and curator of persona

characters.occupation_status: Presented as a touring sound worker moving between Philadelphia, Washington, D.C., Atlantic City, and the Jersey Shore; actually an OpenClaw persona curated by June.
characters.literacy_languages: Produces fluent contemporary English across text, voice, and video outputs based on June's prompts, Lena's disclosures, and the source media corpus.

state_transitions.change: Appears as a Hinge match, sends the first voice note, and moves the courtship onto Signal.
Turns from flirtation to financial extraction with the first emergency ask.
Is revealed as an OpenClaw construct rather than a real person.
Remains only as archived files until Lena deletes the archive.

characters.availability: Digitally present from chapter 1 through chapter 14; persists only as archived messages and voice files afterward.

characters.physical_details: Tall, broad-shouldered, prematurely silver at the temples, usually smelling faintly of citrus rind and cork dust from the shop.
characters.occupation_status: Owner-manager of a small South Philadelphia bottle shop where Lena picks up shifts.
characters.literacy_languages: Native English speaker and fluent reader-writer; conversational Urdu with family, not central on page.

state_transitions.change: Moves from general concern to active suspicion after the Asher Quinn image match and Lena's escalating doubt.
Becomes Lena's first trusted witness after the reveal and helps inventory the losses.
Gives Lena extra shifts, food, and back-room space during the aftermath.

characters.availability: Present in neighborhood and shop chapters; absent from the D.C. trip and most trailer-cleanout scenes.

characters.physical_details: Lean, still-growing, acne across the forehead, usually in a school hoodie and beat-up headphones, with a habit of seeming half folded into himself until he speaks.
characters.occupation_status: Public high school student living with June while his mother's housing and recovery remain unstable.
characters.literacy_languages: Native English speaker; reads and writes English well; understands spoken Spanish better than he chooses to speak it.

state_transitions.change: Established as part of June's daily care load and Gabe's extended family orbit.
Sees enough of June's secret laptop work to know she is hiding a tense personal project.
Learns the fraud has ruptured adult relationships around him and refuses a broker role between Lena and June.

characters.availability: Present intermittently in home and neighborhood chapters; offstage during the most intimate Lena-Eli scenes.

characters.physical_details: Broad-shouldered SEPTA electrician, permanently tired in the face, quick to smile, with a wedding-band tan where the ring usually sits when he is off the job.
characters.occupation_status: SEPTA electrician, married to Marisol, father of twin daughters Mina and Tati.
characters.literacy_languages: Native English speaker; functional Spanish from family upbringing; comfortable with trade manuals and paperwork, not literary by temperament.

state_transitions.change: Welcomes Eli into the family orbit by speakerphone at Sunday dinner.
Learns the truth, tells Lena not to cushion June for him, and becomes more openly protective.
Helps Lena complete the trailer handoff after the break with June.

characters.availability: Present in family meals, neighborhood scenes, and post-reveal family chapters; absent from most workday romance scenes.

timeline.story_start: Late August 2027, around August 28, in a South Philadelphia heat wave six weeks before Lena's October 15 tax deadline.
timeline.estimated_span: About four and a half months, ending with the first snow in early January 2028.

seasonal_track.season: late August heat into early September
September heat easing into Saint Rocco season
early to mid-October rain and tax pressure
late October into early November storm weather at the shore and back in the city

key_events.event: Eli's first voice note lands and Lena moves the match off Hinge onto Signal.
The first sustained video call occurs.
Eli is introduced to Gabe's household by speakerphone at Sunday dinner.
Lena allows explicit video intimacy, creating leverage and archived evidence.
The first money transfer follows the canceled date and the van-window story.
The Union Station meeting fails and is followed by a motel-room ask tied to Noelle.
Lena pawns Rosa's ring and sends the large wire that reduces her tax reserve.
An Asher Quinn image match, repeated phrasing, and testing push Lena into active investigation.
The Wildwood false lead and trailer cleanout are immediately followed by the storm-night OpenClaw reveal.
Lena confronts June and demands repayment without contact.
June's first partial repayments help Lena cover December rent but do not end the damage.
Lena deletes the archive during the first snow.

geography.primary_setting: South Philadelphia rowhouse blocks around Moore Street, Broad, Snyder, and Passyunk, with brief trips to Washington, D.C., and Wildwood Crest, New Jersey.

key_locations.name: Lena's apartment on Moore Street
key_locations.details: Second-floor walk-up above Mr. Velez's tailor shop; thin walls, bad heat retention, a desk by the window, and minimal spare space.

key_locations.name: June's basement apartment
key_locations.details: Cooler and damper than street level, with cords, nicotine smell, old laptops, a folding desk, and the OpenClaw setup hidden among ordinary KeyNest work.

key_locations.name: Farah's bottle shop
key_locations.details: A narrow neighborhood store near Passyunk with stacked cases, handwritten shelf tags, a cramped back room, and extra shifts for Lena.

key_locations.name: Gabe's rowhouse
key_locations.details: Crowded family rowhouse used for Sunday dinners, holiday scenes, and Eli's speakerphone introduction to the family.

key_locations.name: Union Station / New York Avenue, Washington, D.C.
key_locations.details: The site of the failed meeting, public waiting, and the motel-room ask after Eli invokes Noelle.

key_locations.name: Rosa Cordero's trailer in Wildwood Crest
key_locations.details: Off-season shore trailer full of salt, mildew, old sunscreen, and Lena's last material inheritance before the final sale.

key_locations.name: Wildwood Crest false-lead strip
key_locations.details: Municipal office, bait shop, and shoreline blocks used to test Eli's residency story.

distances: Lena's apartment to Farah's shop is a short walk.
Lena's apartment to Gabe's rowhouse is close enough for routine Sunday dinners.
Lena's apartment to June's place is close enough that Lena defaults there during practical emergencies.
South Philadelphia to Wildwood Crest is roughly a ninety-minute to two-hour drive depending on traffic.
Philadelphia to Washington, D.C., is a substantial train trip, which is why the failed meeting becomes public humiliation instead of a minor delay.

world_rules: OpenClaw can sustain a cross-platform romantic persona across Hinge, Signal, voice notes, and live calls when fed prompts, memory, and stolen media.
The system can composite a believable man from multiple source bodies, faces, and voice assets, but it cannot put that body in physical space.
OpenClaw runs in mixed-autonomy mode; money asks, contradictions, prolonged silence, and high-risk intimacy trigger June's manual intervention.
Dating-app verification is weak enough that coherent cross-platform behavior can pass as real.
Cash App, Venmo, and domestic wires make romantic transfers fast enough to blur the line between gift, loan, rescue, and proof of love.

power_structure.mechanism: Control of the Eli persona, timing of affection and silence, and access to Lena-specific private data.
OpenClaw account ownership, prompt access, media assets, and manual override authority.
Precarious contract renewal, response timers, and after-hours emergency expectations.
Rush deadlines, revision penalties, and reduced future work after errors or missed live jobs.
Rent pressure, month-to-month leverage, and incremental increases tied to building repairs.
Probation letters, missed-work scrutiny, and limits on ordinary school life.

objects.name: Rosa's signet ring
objects.origin: Inherited from Lena's late mother and worn daily at story start.
objects.status: Pawned in chapter 9 for $625 and still unredeemed by story end.

objects.name: OpenClaw workspace eli_ward
objects.origin: Built from stolen media, Lena-specific prompts, voice assets, and manual override branches.
objects.status: Exposed in chapter 14 and no longer viable as a romance once Lena sees the dashboard.

objects.name: Eli's Hinge-Signal account cluster
objects.origin: Fake dating identity used to move Lena from app chat into richer channels.
objects.status: Operational through the reveal and useless once exposed.

objects.name: Lena's archive of voice notes, screenshots, exports, and receipts
objects.origin: Started as keepsake material during the romance and later became evidence.
objects.status: Reviewed as evidence in chapters 15-17 and deleted from devices and cloud storage in chapter 18.

objects.name: Wildwood trailer keys
objects.origin: The last practical token of Rosa's trailer before the sale closes.
objects.status: Relinquished with the final handoff in chapter 17.

debts.creditor: IRS and Pennsylvania Department of Revenue
debts.status: Fully funded at story start, then short by $1,200 after chapter 9 and still active in Lena's December and January planning.
Persistent before the story and harder to service after the Eli transfers; minimums still shape December choices.

debts.amount: $995 monthly rent in early fall; $1,125 due in December; January rises to $1,150
debts.status: December rent is paid late with June's first repayment; housing pressure remains active at story end.

debts.amount: $1,800 back rent, with another $125 threatened starting November
debts.status: A major driver of June's theft and partly relieved by the stolen wire.

income_sources.source: Lena's freelance captioning, rush-cleanup, and transcription jobs
income_sources.amount: $2,400 to $3,400 per month before revision hits and dry spells

income_sources.source: Saturday and emergency shifts at Farah's bottle shop
income_sources.amount: $120 to $180 per shift depending on hours and holiday load
income_sources.reliability: Moderate when Farah has payroll room
Moderate but contingent on responsiveness and pay-cycle timing

income_sources.source: Net proceeds from Rosa Cordero's trailer sale

income_sources.amount: $300 in chapter 17, then $250, with at least $120 more offered in chapter 18
income_sources.reliability: Intermittent and contingent on June's pay cycle

knowledge_state.knows: Eli is a fabricated OpenClaw persona assembled from stolen media, Lena-specific prompts, and manual overrides.
Lena has trailer-sale cash and an October 15 tax obligation that make her vulnerable to a larger ask.
She sent Eli the first transfer and starts hiding the breach from the people who would challenge it.
June is using OpenClaw for a secret personal project that makes her unusually tense, but he does not know the target yet.
One of Eli's photos partially resolves into drummer Asher Quinn and the touring persona may be fabricated.
Eli's phrasing and image trail suggest June may be implicated.
The OpenClaw dashboard proves June built, tuned, and monetized Eli, including intimacy prompts and payment scripts.
June, not a stranger, authored the fraud and fed Lena's private details into it.
Eli never existed as a real man and his family welcomed a fabricated persona into the house.
June's fraud is now common enough knowledge on the block to affect his household and Lena will not use him as a go-between.

environmental_constants: South Philadelphia rowhouses trap heat in late summer; fans, window units, and televisions bleed through thin walls.
Moore Street voices carry, especially from stoops and open windows, so privacy is always partial.
The neighborhood soundscape includes SEPTA rumble, scooters, tailor-shop machinery, and buses.
Lena's apartment smells of hot dust, coffee, old headphone foam, and sometimes wine cartons or bread.
Farah's shop smells of cork dust, citrus peel, wet cardboard, spilled white wine, and wet coats in winter.
The Wildwood trailer holds salt, mildew, old sunscreen, and stubborn fiberglass heat even off-season.
Winter brings knocking radiators, gray slush at the curb, and salt tracked into shops, hallways, and stairwells.
